2603.23719.title: CDMT-EHR: A Continuous-Time Diffusion Framework for Generating Mixed-Type Time-Series Electronic Health Records
2603.23719.paper_summary: Electronic health records (EHRs) are invaluable for clinical research, yet privacy concerns severely restrict data sharing. Synthetic data generation offers a promising solution, but EHRs present unique challenges: they contain both numerical and categorical features that evolve over time. While diffusion models have demonstrated strong performance in EHR synthesis, existing approaches predominantly rely on discrete-time formulations, which suffer from finite-step approximation errors and coupled training-sampling step counts. We propose a continuous-time diffusion framework for generating mixed-type time-series EHRs with three contributions: (1) continuous-time diffusion with a bidirectional gated recurrent unit backbone for capturing temporal dependencies, (2) unified Gaussian diffusion via learnable continuous embeddings for categorical variables, enabling joint cross-feature modeling, and (3) a factorized learnable noise schedule that adapts to per-feature-per-timestep learning difficulties. Experiments on two large-scale intensive care unit datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms existing approaches in downstream task performance, distribution fidelity, and discriminability, while requiring only 50 sampling steps compared to 1,000 for baseline methods. Classifier-free guidance further enables effective conditional generation for class-imbalanced clinical scenarios.
2603.23719.paper_summary_zh: 電子健康紀錄 (EHRs) 對臨床研究來說是無價的，但隱私問題嚴重限制了數據共享。合成數據生成提供了一個有前景的解決方案，但 EHRs 具有獨特的挑戰：它們包含隨時間演變的數值和類別特徵。雖然擴散模型在 EHR 合成中表現出色，但現有的方法主要依賴於離散時間的公式，這會遭受有限步驟近似誤差和耦合的訓練-取樣步驟計數。我們提出了一個連續時間擴散框架，用於生成混合型時間序列 EHRs，並有三個貢獻：（1）使用雙向門控遞迴單元骨幹的連續時間擴散，以捕捉時間依賴性，（2）通過可學習的連續嵌入實現類別變數的統一高斯擴散，從而實現跨特徵的聯合建模，以及（3）一個可分解的可學習噪音時間表，能夠適應每個特徵每個時間步的學習難度。在兩個大型重症監護病房數據集上的實驗表明，我們的方法在下游任務性能、分佈保真度和可區分性方面超過了現有的方法，同時只需 50 個取樣步驟，相較於基線方法的 1,000 個步驟。無分類器引導進一步促進了對類別不平衡臨床場景的有效條件生成。
2603.23719.author: Shaonan Liu et.al.
2603.23719.authors: Shaonan Liu, Yuichiro Iwashita, Soichiro Nakako, Masakazu Iwamura, Koichi Kise
